items.title: Oil Traders Crowd Around Cheap TACO Hedge as Iran War Escalates
items.description: An unusual options trade is becoming a favored hedge against the risks of sudden U-turns in US President Donald Trump’s stance on Iran, a dynamic that has repeatedly whipsawed markets in recent months

items.title: Podcast: Tokenization, Digital Twins and the Future of ETFs
items.description: On this episode of Trillions, we explore how this government transformation will affect stocks, bonds and exchange-traded funds.

items.title: Turning Oil to Fuel Has Never Been More Profitable as Refining Margins Surge
items.description: Profit margins for US refiners making gasoline and diesel from crude oil are shattering records, as fuel supply disruptions driven by war lift prices for refined products.

items.title: Citi Traders Pitch Shorting French Shipping Giant CMA CGM Bonds
items.description: Citigroup’s credit traders and analysts are recommending their hedge fund clients short bonds issued by French shipping giant CMA CGM Group, according to people familiar with the matter.

items.title: Paschi Casts Doubts Over Intesa Bid, Suggests Studying BPM Plan

items.title: Chevron to Sign Oil Investment Accords With Iraq on Friday
items.description: Chevron Corp. expects to sign accords with Iraq on Friday that will advance its negotiations about investing in large oil fields, according to a senior company executive.

items.title: Balbec Secures $930 Million in Commitments for Asset-Based Credit Fund
items.description: Alternative asset manager Balbec Capital LP has obtained commitments of more than $930 million for its latest flagship fund that will invest in a variety of asset-based credit, according to a filing o

items.title: India’s Maruti to Fight Order to Replace Car Over Ethanol Damage
items.description: Maruti Suzuki India Ltd. said it will challenge a consumer court order asking the nation’s largest carmaker to replace a vehicle over alleged damage from ethanol-blended fuel, a case that assumes sign

items.title: Aston Martin Creditors Tap Jefferies as Debt Worries Grow
items.description: A group of creditors to Aston Martin Lagonda Global Holdings Plc have picked Jefferies Financial Group Inc. as financial adviser as concern over the debt of the distressed British automaker grows, acc

items.title: Bank of America Exits Chicago Plan to Sell Overdue Parking Debt
items.description: Bank of America is no longer working with Chicago on the sale of debt owed to the city from fines and fees, and officials have been unable to advance the deal so far this year, according to the mayor’

items.title: Ladbrokes Owner Entain to Cut 2% of Staff as UK Taxes Hit
items.description: Entain Plc will cut 500 jobs, or about 2% of its global workforce, as the gambling industry grapples with higher UK taxes, tighter regulation and growing competition from prediction markets.

items.title: Bond Traders Bail on Fed Hike Wagers on Softer Inflation Outlook

items.title: What Nigeria Stocks Tell Investors About Africa Markets: Live Q&A
items.description: Bloomberg reporters answer your questions about Nigeria’s world-beating stocks, and where investors are finding opportunities in African markets, in a Live Q&A on Friday, July 17 at 12 p.m. GMT

items.title: Faena Condo Project Sells $30 Million Penthouse on Miami River
items.description: Argentine fashion entrepreneur-turned-developer Alan Faena sold a $30 million penthouse in his planned twin condo towers on the Miami River, a milestone in the mission to take his maximalist, theatric

items.title: Multistrat Hedge Fund Paloma to Cut Teams in Latest Pivot
items.description: Donald Sussman, the veteran behind Paloma Partners, is pivoting his long-standing multistrategy hedge fund to focus on fewer managers in which he has the highest conviction as the firm adjusts to a de

items.title: Emerging Currencies Dip as Oil, Dollar Spike Over Iran Worries
items.description: Most developing-world currencies fell Thursday as renewed worries about tensions in the Middle East lifted oil prices in another risk-off session in global markets.

items.title: Wheat Wavers After Touching Two-Year High Amid Black Sea Strikes
items.description: Wheat fluctuated after touching a two-year high as traders weighed ongoing Ukrainian and Russian strikes in the Black Sea and weak US export data.

items.title: Gold Falls as US-Iran Hostilities Rekindles Fed Hike Bets
items.description: Gold declined as escalating tensions in the Middle East rekindled expectations that the Federal Reserve may need to hike interest rates to contain stubborn inflation.

items.title: Chip Stocks Hit by AI Spending Worries After Rally: Markets Wrap
items.description: A selloff in chipmakers dragged down stocks amid concerns over whether massive artificial-intelligence investments will justify lofty valuations, with a resurgence in geopolitical risks also weighing

items.title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 16
items.description: Oil steadied, largely holding onto a run of gains with concerns mounting about disruptions to flows from the Middle East as US strikes against Iran intensify.
